body:
## Summary

**Bit-rot detection and repair are silently broken for the `FsChunkStore` backend** (the only on-disk backend ‚Äî dev/NAS *and* the interim production networked D server, per ADR-0032). A `ChunkStore::get_fragment` contract mismatch makes the scrub pass **abort** on the first corrupt fragment, *before* the branch that would enqueue it for repair ever runs ‚Äî so a checksum-failing fragment is never turned into a repair obligation, contrary to scrub's documented guarantee. Worse, the durability telemetry reads all-green while data rots.

This is a core M3 durability guarantee not holding. High severity.

## Root cause ‚Äî `get_fragment` corruption contract mismatch

Two reasonable behaviours that are broken in combination:

- **`FsChunkStore::get_fragment` verifies on read and returns `Err` for a corrupt/misfiled fragment** (`crates/chunkstore-fs/src/lib.rs:87-97`, `Self::verify(id, &bytes)?`; `verify` at `:53-66` checks valid decode **and** `header.chunk_id`/`index == id`). It returns `Ok(None)` only for *absent*, `Ok(Some)` only for *valid*.
- **Scrub assumes `get_fragment` returns raw bytes for it to check**, then verifies with `repair::fragment_intact` (`crates/custodian/src/scrub.rs:70-85`):

```rust
let Some(bytes) = store.get_fragment(frag).await? else { continue };   // :70  <-- ? propagates Err
emit_scrubbed(dserver, frag);                                         // :75
if !repair::fragment_intact(&bytes, frag.chunk) {                     // :79
    emit_corruption(dserver, frag);                                  // :82
    repair::enqueue_repair(ctx.meta, frag.chunk, "scrub").await?;    // :83
}
```

When scrub hits a bit-rotten fragment, `store.get_fragment(frag).await?` returns `Err` and the **`?` propagates out of the whole `reconcile` pass** ‚Äî before `fragment_intact` (`:79`) and `enqueue_repair` (`:83`).

## Mechanism / impact

1. **The corruption branch is dead code for this backend.** `FsChunkStore::verify` already checks what `fragment_intact` checks (valid decode + `header.chunk_id == chunk`), so any bytes that reach `:79` have already passed ‚Äî `if !fragment_intact` can never be true here.
2. **Bit rot is never enqueued for repair** ‚Äî the opposite of the module's contract ("a checksum-failing fragment is never absorbed silently ‚Äî it always becomes a durable repair obligation").
3. **Deterministic and self-perpetuating:** the pass aborts at the *first* corrupt fragment in iteration order every pass, so scrub never reaches the fragments after it. One rotted fragment stalls scrub for that entire D server.
4. **The telemetry lies.** `emit_scrubbed` (`:75`) and `emit_corruption` (`:82`) both sit *after* the aborting `get`, so `scrub_coverage` silently plateaus and `scrub_corruption_rate` stays **0** while data rots ‚Äî precisely the "a storage system silently below its redundancy floor reports all-green" failure mode the design warns about (`docs/design/architecture/08-crosscutting-concepts.md:50`).

Holds through the network path too: server `FsChunkStore` `Err` ‚Üí `Status::internal` ‚Üí client `GrpcChunkStore::get_fragment` `Err` ‚Üí scrub's `?` aborts.

## Contributing facet ‚Äî collapsed error classification

Even a corruption-aware scrub couldn't branch correctly today:

- the server maps **all** store errors to `Status::internal` (`crates/chunkstore-grpc/src/server.rs:46-60`), and the client lumps everything non-`Unavailable`/`DeadlineExceeded` into a catch-all `Rpc` (`crates/chunkstore-grpc/src/error.rs:30-38`). There is **no `DATA_LOSS` code and no `TransportError::Corrupt` variant**, so "corrupt ‚Üí repair" is indistinguishable from "transient ‚Üí retry."
- Separately, a client's malformed-fragment **PUT** is also reported as `INTERNAL` (a server fault) when it is really `INVALID_ARGUMENT` ‚Äî inviting futile retries of a put that will always fail verify.

## Fix direction

Settle the contract so the store never serves corrupt bytes **and** corruption is actionable:

- keep `get_fragment` verifying, but return a **distinguishable corrupt error** (not a generic boxed error); the server maps it to `Code::DataLoss`; add a `TransportError::Corrupt` variant.
- scrub treats a corrupt-get as **`enqueue_repair` + continue**, never `?`-propagate ‚Äî so corruption becomes a repair obligation and the pass keeps going (and `emit_corruption`/coverage stay honest).
- reclassify PUT verify failures as `INVALID_ARGUMENT`, reserving `INTERNAL`/`DATA_LOSS` for genuine I/O faults.

## Definition of done

- [ ] a corrupt/misfiled fragment causes scrub to **enqueue its chunk for repair and continue** the pass ‚Äî never aborts it
- [ ] regression test: a D server with one bit-rotten referenced fragment ‚Üí scrub enqueues that chunk for reconstruction, emits a corruption metric, and still scrubs the remaining fragments
- [ ] `get_fragment` corruption is a distinguishable error (server `DATA_LOSS`; `TransportError::Corrupt`); transient faults stay distinct
- [ ] PUT verify failures surface as `INVALID_ARGUMENT`, not `INTERNAL`
- [ ] confirm/repair the **read path** (`crates/core/src/read.rs`): verify its corrupt-fragment repair-trigger is actually reachable given the store verifies on read (the same mismatch may make its `decode`-failure path unreachable) ‚Äî adjacent to #198

## What is correct (scope boundary)

The wire/encoding layers are sound ‚Äî no truncation/aliasing bugs: chunk id crosses as `fixed64` hi/lo halves with correct reassembly and the index uses `u16::try_from` ‚Üí `INVALID_ARGUMENT` (`crates/chunkstore-grpc/src/conv.rs:14-49`); `GetFragment` uses `optional bytes` so absent ‚âÝ empty (`crates/proto/proto/wyrd/v0/chunk.proto:37-41`); the client preserves `None` (`crates/chunkstore-grpc/src/client.rs:73-75`). The bug is specifically at the verify-contract seam.

## Refs

- `crates/custodian/src/scrub.rs:54,62,70-85` (the aborting `?`, the unreached corruption branch)
- `crates/chunkstore-fs/src/lib.rs:53-66,87-97` (`verify`, `get_fragment` returns `Err` on corruption)
- `crates/chunkstore-grpc/src/server.rs:46-60` (all store errors ‚Üí `INTERNAL`); `crates/chunkstore-grpc/src/error.rs:30-38` (catch-all classification)
- `docs/design/architecture/08-crosscutting-concepts.md:50` (¬ß8.3 "reports all-green" failure mode)
- Related: #198 (read-path `chunk_id` recheck asymmetry), #203 (temp-path race), #204/#205 (d-server parallelism), ADR-0032 (FsChunkStore is dev/NAS + interim production backend)

milestone.title: M3 ‚Äî Custodians
milestone.description: The self-maintaining durability plane (L4): GC, scrub, reconstruction, rebalance + durability telemetry. Makes single-zone trustworthy; gates the Step-2 release. Plan: proposal 0005.

title: Scrub cannot detect or repair bit rot: get_fragment corruption error aborts the pass before the repair branch
